free_text: Cross sections and thermonuclear reaction rates for
Ca-42(p,gamma)Sc-43, Ca-44(p,gamma)Sc-45,
Ca-44(p,n)Sc-44 and Sc-45(p,n)Ti-45
Thermonuclear reaction rates. Statistical model
analysis.
The uncertainty in the detector calibration.

SD:Updated to new date formats, lower case.
Corrections in all Subents. Data were re-digitized.
SD: Small corrections in all Subents.
Targets of metallic Ca were prepared by evaporation
from a baffled tantalum oven containing a mixture of
isotopically enriched CaCO(3) and tantalum powder.
The CaCO(3) used in the preparation of the Ca-44
targets was enriched to 98.55% in Ca-44. The target
was deposited onto a 0.25 mm tantalum substrate and
allowed to oxidize fully in air before bombardment.
The cross section have been deduced from gamma-rays
yield.
Statistical uncertainty

Targets of metallic Ca were prepared by evaporation
from a baffled tantalum oven containing a mixture of
isotopically enriched CaCO(3) and tantalum powder.
The CaCO(3) used in the preparation of the Ca-42
targets was enriched to 94.42% in Ca-42. The target
was deposited on etched tungsten backing and was
transferred to the target chamber under argon to
minimize oxidation because thick, oxidized Ca targets
to peel off the backing.
The cross section have been deduced from gamma-rays
yield.
Statistical uncertainty
